title: Cost Analyst (Manufacturing)
description:
This role leads cost analysis within a manufacturing environment, focusing on COGS, production costs, and inventory valuation. The analyst will partner closely with Production and Procurement to drive cost transparency, control, and improvement.

Key Responsibilities

  • Cost Accounting & Variance Review
    Analyze monthly actual vs. standard costs, investigate material/labor/overhead variances, and provide clear insights with recommended actions.

  • Production Cost & Performance Analysis
    Assess material usage, labor efficiency, machine utilization, and overhead absorption. Track manufacturing KPIs and highlight cost drivers affecting gross profit.

  • Inventory Valuation & Standard Costing
    Ensure accurate inventory valuation, proper COGS flow, and effective management of manufacturing variances. Support regular standard cost reviews.

  • Budgeting, Forecasting & Decision Support
    Provide cost assumptions, GP projections, and analysis for budgets, forecasts, CapEx, and cost‑benefit studies. Identify opportunities to optimize cost and margin.

  • Process & System Improvements
    Strengthen ERP costing processes, improve BOM and cost accuracy, and drive continuous improvement in costing methodologies and reporting.

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s in Accounting/Finance/Economics
  • 3–5 years of cost or management accounting experience in manufacturing industry
  • Solid understanding of COGS, manufacturing processes, and margin drivers
